title: Phase 1 Wave 0 Task 1: GeoAnchor types (IN PROGRESS)
description: packages/geo/types.ts — GeoAnchor discriminated union {kind:'point'|'bbox'|'polyline',...,zRef:number} + GeoCustomData with projection:'mercator'. Shape confirmed by E-03 resolution. Handoff: file not yet created; cross-reference packages/geo/types.ts per Phase 1 plan artifact manifest.

title: Phase 1 Wave 0 Task 2: AtlasdrawTool interface (IN PROGRESS)
description: packages/tools/src/types.ts — AtlasdrawTool interface skeleton. Implementing agent should follow Phase 1 plan Wave 0 task spec.

title: HELD: GitHub org decision required before remote push
description: Wave 5 Task 13 (Phase 1) blocked on maintainer choosing GitHub org for atlasdraw/atlasdraw. Affects: git remote set-url origin, first push. No code change needed — pure infra decision.

title: HELD: Maintainer decision on E-01 Yjs E2EE option (A/B/C)
description: Gate condition for Phase 5 Task 8 wiring beyond stub. Options: (A) server-trusted no layer E2EE, (B) custom log-replay relay ~+1 week, (C) defer to Phase 6. Recommended: Option C. See docs/decisions/escalations.md E-01.

title: HELD: E-02 gate — DiffEngine dependency on E-01 resolution
description: Phase 7 Tasks 9 (SnapshotStore) and 10 (DiffEngine) assume plaintext Y.Doc. If E-01 resolves as Option B, DiffEngine needs decryptSnapshot step. May proceed assuming Option A/C until E-01 formally closed. See escalations.md E-02.

title: HELD: E-03 GeoAnchor shape confirmation before Phase 3/5 serialization tasks
description: Maintainer must confirm authoritative GeoAnchor shape is Phase 1 discriminated union (not flat objects in Phase 3/5 consumer tables). Phase 3 serialization tasks and Phase 5 GeoAnchor-consuming tasks blocked until confirmed. See escalations.md E-03 and cross-phase-audit.md MISMATCH-1/3/5.
closeReason: outcome:success — E-03 GeoAnchor shape confirmed empirically by Phase 1 Wave 0+3 implementation (discriminated union shipped via @atlasdraw/geo, consumed by PinTool/useGeoAnchor/seedToElement; E2E tests verify shape stability across pan)

title: HELD: Manual corepack enable required in real shell
description: Run 'corepack enable && corepack prepare yarn@1.22.22 --activate' manually in atlasdraw repo shell before any CI agent runs yarn install. Cannot be automated in sandboxed execution environments.

title: Phase 1 Waves 1-4: Geo Foundation execution (weeks 2-5)
description: Execute Phase 1 plan Waves 1-4: CoordinateSync, BasemapRegistry, MapEditor, PinTool, event-routing hardening, benchmark spike. Gate: bench/results/phase-1-baseline.json must exist before Phase 2 begins. E2E browser matrix required: Chrome/Firefox/Safari/iOS Safari/Android Chrome.

title: Phase 2: Tools + Data Layers (weeks 6-8)
description: 7 tools (Polygon, Line, Area, Measure, GPX, KML, GeoJSON importers), LayerRegistry Zustand slice, @placemarkio/check-geojson replacing geojsonhint, @mapbox/togeojson replacing togeojson. Gate: phase-1-baseline.json exists.

title: Phase 3: File Format .atlasdraw (week 9)
description: AtlasdrawDocument type, read/write/PersistenceStore, parseCSV. IndexedDB primary path, FSA Chromium-only enhancement. E-03 gate must be closed before serialization tasks touching GeoAnchor.

title: Phase 4: MVP self-host + Docker (weeks 10-11)
description: StorageClient, docker-compose.yml (5-svc with profiles guard), docker-compose.minimal.yml (3-svc), bundled PMTiles world-low-zoom.pmtiles zoom 0-6, /m/:uuid share route, size-limit CI. Fastify v5.8.x. Share endpoint needs adversarial-api-testing annotation.

title: Phase 5: Realtime collaboration (weeks 12-15)
description: Socket.IO + y-websocket dual protocol on separate connections. yjs-crypto.ts stub only — E-01 gate blocks wiring. In-memory TTL eviction (ROOM_TTL_MS=300000). Redis adapter key 'atlasdraw:sio'. E2E test: 5MB Yjs initial state, assert cursor >30fps during catch-up.

title: Phase 6: v1.0 SDK + embeds + comments + hosted mode (weeks 16-25)
description: AtlasdrawAPI postMessage-safe (ADR 0005), Felt importer packages/data/felt.ts, Stripe hooks, workspace abstraction, ADR 0006 telemetry, style editor, comment Yjs doc, setPersistence wiring from Phase 5 stub. Stripe webhook tasks need adversarial-api-testing (GAP-1). No machine-readable artifact manifest block (GAP-3).

title: Phase 7: v1.5 field plugins + AI styling (weeks 26+)
description: PluginRegistry, AIStyleClient (OpenAI Chat Completions shape for Ollama/BYOK), Worker sandbox prelude, SnapshotStore, DiffEngine (gate E-02). PluginRegistry.install() needs adversarial-api-testing gate (GAP-2). SPDX field required in PluginManifest.

title: Cleanup: orphan workflow cancel.yml has hardcoded Excalidraw repo IDs
description: .github/workflows/cancel.yml contains hardcoded Excalidraw repo IDs from upstream. Replace with atlasdraw repo IDs or delete if cancel workflow not needed. Phase 1 cleanup wave.

title: Doc debt: tech-spec.md §10 still says OpenFreeMap is default basemap
description: Q3 resolved basemap default as hybrid (PMTiles bundled, OpenFreeMap demo-only). Spec §10 not updated. No phase plan has a task for this. Fix before Phase 4 execution to avoid confusion. See cross-phase-audit.md GAP-5.

title: Add adversarial-api-testing gate to Phase 6 Stripe webhook tasks
description: Stripe webhook signing verification is a known attack surface (replay, spoofed signature). Phase 6 webhook tasks lack adversarial-api-testing annotation. Add post-implementation gate task. See cross-phase-audit.md GAP-1.

title: Add observability to apps/storage and apps/realtime for hosted instance
description: No phase plans structured error logging, health endpoints, or distributed tracing. Phase 4 demo has no observability. Add before Show HN / hosted launch. See cross-phase-audit.md GAP-6.

title: Wave 2: deep parser parseGeoCustomData (replace shallow isGeoCustomData guard)
description: isGeoCustomData currently does shallow type-guard with no validation of geo.kind discriminator. Wave 2 must land parseGeoCustomData(value: unknown): Result<GeoCustomData, ParseError> before any consumer trusts persisted/imported customData. Source: opus-audit-2026-05-04.md risk #2; reaffirmed in this session's audit follow-up.

title: Wave 2: schemaVersion migration shim (identity migrate even at v1)
description: GeoCustomData.schemaVersion: 1 has no migration scaffold. First schema bump will leave every persisted file unhandled. Add migrate(value: unknown, fromVersion: number): GeoCustomData even as identity at v1 so call sites are wired and v2 only adds a branch. Source: opus-audit-2026-05-04.md risk #5.

title: Declare react peerDependency on @atlasdraw/tools
description: packages/tools imports React types implicitly. Works in monorepo via hoisting, breaks on external publish. Add peerDependencies: { react: '>=18' } to packages/tools/package.json — basemap already declares this. Source: opus-audit-2026-05-04.md risk #3.

title: Validate zRef bounds at CoordinateSync boundary (0 <= zRef <= maxZoom)
description: zRef typed as plain number — accepts negative, NaN, Infinity, fractional values that maplibre rejects silently. Validate in parseGeoCustomData and at CoordinateSync.geoToScene/sceneToGeo entry. Source: opus-audit-2026-05-04.md risk #4.

title: Triage 4 contract drifts found by Wave 1 contracts cleanup
description: Contracts worker found 4 additional drifts beyond D-GEO-1/D-TOOLS-4/6: (1) GeoCustomData typed interface in contract vs type alias in impl; (2) AtlasdrawTool.icon: React.FC in contract vs string in impl; impl also adds required readonly label: string field; (3) onDoubleClick optional method in contract not in impl; (4) id: string in contract vs readonly id: string in impl. Need verdict per drift on contract-vs-impl-canonical. Block Phase 3+ tool authoring until resolved.

title: Wave 1 Task 4 incomplete — syncMapToScene orchestrator missing from CoordinateSync
description: Wave 1 Task 4 spec (plan line 400) required: 'Implement syncMapToScene() with correct captureUpdate: "never". Implement projectElement() as a switch on geo.kind — point arm working, bbox and polyline arms returning {...el} stubs.' Worker B shipped CoordinateSync skeleton + per-element helpers (geoToScene/sceneToGeo) but skipped the orchestrator method. This is incomplete Wave 1 work, not naming drift. The orchestrator owns load-bearing contracts: <8ms p95 perf budget for 5k elements, captureUpdate:never undo invariant, 16ms rAF throttle. Tasks 5/6/7 (Wave 2a/b/c) are designed to add switch arms in parallel — they cannot run until the switch + orchestrator exist. Scope: ~50-80 lines of impl reading scene via excalidrawAPI.getSceneElements(), iterating geo-anchored elements through projectElement, and a single batched updateScene({captureUpdate: 'never'}) call. Land as Wave 1.5 before Wave 2 dispatch.
closeReason: outcome:success — Wave 1 Task 4 completed: syncMapToScene + private projectElement switch (point arm working, bbox/polyline stubs); constructor refactored to {map, excalidrawAPI} per contract; invented per-anchor API (geoToScene/sceneToGeo/subscribeToMapChanges) removed (zero consumers, confirmed by monorepo typecheck + doc sweep); tests A/B/C land per plan Step 1; folded atlasdraw-23a5

title: Wave 1 TDD bypass — write CoordinateSync tests A/B/C or file explicit waiver
description: Phase 1 plan invokes test-driven-development skill with Step 1 = write failing unit tests A/B/C. Wave 1 shipped zero tests. Either land the three test fixtures before Wave 2, or formally waive TDD with rationale (skeletons-first justification).
closeReason: outcome:rework — folded into atlasdraw-68e5; tests A/B/C land as Step 1 of completing Wave 1 Task 4 syncMapToScene impl per plan TDD assignment

title: Task 3 dropped sources: BasemapRegistry, pmtiles-protocol, style-builder, MapCanvas.test.tsx
description: Phase 1 Task 3 plan mandated BasemapRegistry.ts, pmtiles-protocol.ts, style-builder.ts, __tests__/MapCanvas.test.tsx, and a pmtiles dependency. None present after Wave 1. packages/basemap/package.json description still advertises all four. Either land the missing pieces in Wave 2 or update package.json + plan to reflect skeleton scope. Adds: (1) basemap/package.json description currently misleading.

title: Demo install regressed monorepo typecheck (TS5107 moduleResolution=node10)
description: Demo agent yarn install for apps/atlas-app upgraded TypeScript via vite peer-deps. Now yarn test:typecheck fails monorepo-wide on TS5107 (deprecated moduleResolution=node10). Fix: either add ignoreDeprecations: 5.0 to tsconfig.base.json, OR pin TS version in root yarn.lock, OR migrate base config to moduleResolution: bundler. Worker A's earlier exit-0 typecheck is no longer reproducible. Opus follow-up audit ran BEFORE the regression so its 'typecheck:0' is stale. Blocking Wave 2.

title: Task 8: scaleMode override + hybrid clamp — per-kind override logic
description: Wave 2 shipped Tasks 5/6/7/9/10. Task 8 deferred — current _projectElement matches spec §3.4 defaults for Phase 1 demos (point=screen via untouched width/height, bbox/polyline=geographic via natural projection scaling). Non-default combos need explicit override: point+geographic|hybrid scales el.width/height by computeScaleFactor; bbox+screen overrides projected span with stored; bbox+hybrid clamps span 2^-2..2^+2; polyline+screen|hybrid similar. Spec §3.4 default for arrow/freehand is hybrid — those tools cannot ship correctly without this. Add scaleMode.ts helper + wire into _projectElement when first non-default consumer lands. See CoordinateSync.ts _projectElement docstring.

title: Task 9 Step 4: syncSceneToMap inverse for point anchor (gated on PinTool consumer)
description: Wave 1.5 deferred this; Wave 2 Task 9 Steps 1-3 (fast-check + property tests) shipped standalone. syncSceneToMap is inverse direction (re-derive customData.geo from el.x/el.y after user-drag). Implement point-kind first when Task 14 (PinTool) lands — first real consumer (pointerup -> new lng/lat from drag). Include captureUpdate (likely 'immediately' for user-driven). Round-trip property test in projection.property.test.ts is the correctness gate.

title: Excalidraw viewBackgroundColor footgun: NOT a top-level prop — lives in AppState
description: Wave 3 Task 11 shipped <Excalidraw viewBackgroundColor='transparent'/>. Prop silently ignored — viewBackgroundColor is an AppState field (default COLOR_PALETTE.white). Canvas painted opaque white, hiding MapLibre. Caught only by visual screenshot — typecheck/build BOTH passed. Fix: initialData={{appState:{viewBackgroundColor:'transparent'}}}. Promote to mulch architecture: 'Excalidraw v0.18 ExcalidrawProps (types.ts:570) is the source of truth for top-level props; AppState fields (viewBackgroundColor, gridSize, theme, etc.) MUST go via initialData.appState not as direct props. TS allows unknown props silently. Verify visually before declaring Excalidraw integration done.'
closeReason: outcome:success — promoted to mulch excalidraw-integration domain. 4 records recorded: (1) convention 'grep-vendored-excalidraw-source-before-naming-API' (foundational), (2) failure 'viewBackgroundColor-is-AppState-not-prop', (3) failure 'customTools-prop-does-not-exist-in-v0.18', (4) failure 'newElementWith-mutates-not-creates'. Project rule at .claude/rules/excalidraw-api.md codifies the meta-rule for path-scoped enforcement.

title: Wave 3b: decide auto-anchor scope for stock Excalidraw tools (rectangle vs arrow/freedraw)
description: Plan Task 15 ('rectangle stays glued') implicitly requires an onChange auto-anchor that stamps GeoCustomData on stock-tool-created elements. Question: should ALL stock Excalidraw tools auto-anchor, or only bbox-shaped ones (rectangle, ellipse, diamond, polygon)? Spec §3.4 defaults arrow + freedraw to scaleMode:hybrid — those depend on Task 8 (atlasdraw-375a, deferred) for correct projection. A blind 'auto-anchor everything' path creates elements that render wrong on first zoom. Decide before implementing Wave 3b: (a) auto-anchor only bbox tools (rectangle/ellipse/diamond/polygon) with scaleMode:geographic; defer arrow/freedraw to a 2nd path that activates after Task 8 lands. (b) auto-anchor everything with scaleMode:hybrid as a soft mode, accepting visual rough edges until Task 8. (c) skip auto-anchor entirely — only Task 14 PinTool is geo-aware in Phase 1; Task 15 rectangle test requires a different verification mechanism. Resolve at Wave 3b kickoff.
closeReason: outcome:success — option (a) chosen: useGeoAnchor hook auto-stamps customData.geo on rectangle/ellipse/diamond with scaleMode:'geographic'. arrow/freedraw/polygon deferred to post-Task-8 (atlasdraw-375a). Wired in MapEditor.tsx; verified via Playwright E2E rectangle-stays-glued test.

title: Wave 3b BLOCKER: selection tool can't click geo-elements (pointer-events fall through to map)
description: classifyTool('selection') returns false → isDrawingMode=false → .excalidrawLayer has pointer-events:none → events fall through to MapLibre. Once Wave 3b Task 14 ships PinTool, the FIRST user interaction post-pin-creation is: switch to selection tool, click pin to move/delete. This will fail — the click goes to map (pan), not to Excalidraw (select). Surfaces immediately in the demo, NOT Phase 4. Decision menu: (a) classifyTool('selection')=true (selection becomes drawing-mode) — only hand stays pass-through. Simple, predictable. Cost: user can't pan map while selection is active. (b) Hybrid: pointer-events conditional on whether geo-elements exist in scene. Cost: complexity, race conditions on element-add. (c) Custom 'select-geo' tool added; 'selection' stays pass-through. Cost: extra UI affordance + atlasdraw needs to register a custom tool. (d) Combo: selection captures only over geo-element bounding boxes (CSS pointer-events: auto on element shapes; none elsewhere). Cost: requires DOM mirroring of canvas hits. Resolve at Wave 3b kickoff before Worker 14 dispatch. Likely (a) for Phase 1 simplicity; revisit in Phase 4 toolbar work.
closeReason: outcome:success — classifyTool('hand') is now the only pass-through tool. selection becomes drawing-mode → Excalidraw captures clicks on geo-elements. Trade-off: keyboard 'h' or hand button required to pan map. Revisit in Phase 4 toolbar UX.

title: Verify drag-pan vs zoom element behavior matches spec
description: User visual demo (2026-05-04): R, H, Pin all work; rectangle + pin render. Reported: 'they dont move on drag but they move on mouse scroll or zoom. if expected then disregard.' Ambiguous — (a) BUG: elements stay screen-fixed during drag-pan (useCoordinateSync move handler not firing under drag); (b) EXPECTED: stay glued during drag, visibly re-project on zoom (correct). E2E verifies programmatic panBy works. Investigation: reproduce drag and observe element pixel position behavior; if stays fixed, debug throttle under continuous move events.
closeReason: outcome:success — root cause: Excalidraw layer (pointer-events:auto in drawing mode) captured wheel events before MapLibre's scrollZoom listener. User's perception of 'annos drift on zoom' was actually 'map never zoomed' because wheel never reached MapLibre. Fix: useMapWheelRouter hook intercepts wheel in capture phase and routes to map.easeTo (canonical scrollZoom math), preserving ctrl/meta-wheel for browser zoom. Verified by 4 new E2E tests: pin+rect glued under programmatic zoom (drift 0px); pin glued under interactive wheel zoom in both hand mode AND drawing mode (drift 0px). Touch pinch out of scope (pointer-event path, Phase 4).

title: HELD: Webkit system deps required for cross-browser matrix
description: Wave 4 Task 17 cross-browser run: Chromium + Firefox PASS (12/12 E2E). Webkit binary installed but launch fails with: 'Host system is missing dependencies to run browsers'. Required: sudo yarn playwright install-deps (or apt: libicu74 libxml2 libmanette-0.2-0). Cannot be auto-installed (sudo). Once installed, re-run: yarn workspace @atlasdraw/atlas-app e2e:all. If webkit reveals real bugs (e.g. wheel deltaMode quirks not normalized by useMapWheelRouter, pointer-events propagation gaps), file as separate seeds. Until then Phase 1 acceptance is complete on automated chromium + firefox; webkit is the one outstanding automated column. See docs/test-matrix/phase-1.md.
